subject: Human Resources
title: Manager Relationships
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Manager Relationships.

hook.text: People don't leave companies. They leave managers. It's the most-cited reason for voluntary turnover.

buildup.text: The manager relationship is the single strongest predictor of engagement—more than pay, perks, or prestige.

discovery.text: Gallup found that managers account for 70% of the variance in team engagement scores.

twist.text: Most managers were promoted for technical skills—not people skills. They were never taught how to engage a team.

climax.text: Investing in manager training—coaching, feedback, empathy—multiplies engagement across entire organizations.

punchline.text: Fix the managers and you fix engagement. It starts there.

quiz.question: What percentage of engagement variance do managers account for?
